description_long: This is a large, irregularly shaped stone with a rough, granular surface and a muted grayish-tan coloration. The stone features a prominent concave depression on one side, giving it a partially hollowed appearance, while the rest of its body is solid and blocky. Its surface is uneven, with natural pitting, subtle ridges, and a few small, lighter patches that suggest minor mineral inclusions or weathering marks. The overall form is asymmetrical, with one end more rounded and the other more squared off, making it visually distinct from all angles. The stone's texture is coarse and unpolished, indicating it has been exposed to the elements for some time. This type of stone is commonly found as a barrier or obstacle in urban parks, along sidewalks, or as part of landscaping features.
description: A large, rough, grayish-tan stone with an irregular shape and a deep concave depression on one side. Its coarse, weathered surface and asymmetrical form make it a natural-looking urban barrier.
description_view_0: The stone's concave depression is most visible, with rough, pitted texture and rounded edges.
description_view_1: Shows the stone's elongated, blocky side with a mostly flat surface and minimal curvature.
description_view_2: Highlights the stone's asymmetrical profile, with the depression partially visible and the surface appearing rugged.
description_view_3: The concave area is fully exposed, revealing the depth and roughness of the hollowed section.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular granular texture with subtle pitting and occasional lighter mineral spots; no artificial carvings or regular patterns.
attribute_weathering: Rough and unpolished, with visible signs of natural weathering and minor surface erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Blends naturally with soil, gravel, or other landscaping stones; may contrast with paved surfaces but integrates well in green or naturalized urban settings.
